commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh But cursed be the deceiver, who has in his flock a male, and vows, and sacrifices unto the Lord a corrupt thing: for I am a great King, says the LORD of hosts, and my name is feared among the nations. So better things are styled mascula thura. (Virgil; Pliny, xii. ) It was unlawful to offer a female by vow, but not out of devotion, Leviticus xxii. (Calmet) King. So the Persian monarchs were called. Greek, "Epiphanes.
